text: This is the gallbladder that I recently did. I first began by trying to isolate the cystic duct like I normally would. However, I could not get a ICG backfill into the duct. And I decided to do a dome down technique to hopefully help with this backfill in case it was just a delay factor. But as I dissected further, the cystic duct again did not fill. And this suggests that there could be a cystic duct stone

The surgeon has skeletonized the cystic duct, revealing a potential obstruction. The gallbladder is clipped off, and the surgeon prepares to transect just below the clip, observing for bile reflux to assess the presence of obstruction.

The surgeon switches instruments and visually identifies a filling defect in the duct. They gently manipulate the duct with counter traction and milk the proximal duct into the open lumen, taking care not to crush the stone and risk it entering the main bile duct.
